Tests for `Math.max` function behavior with various inputs, including `NaN`, objects, and zero values.


## Files
- **[15.08.02.11-001.js](15.08.02.11-001.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.max` when passed `undefined`, expecting a result of `NaN`.
- **[15.08.02.11-002.js](15.08.02.11-002.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of Math.max when given an object, expecting a result of NaN.
- **[15.08.02.11-003.js](15.08.02.11-003.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.max` returns `NaN` when called with an empty object.
- **[15.08.02.11-004.js](15.08.02.11-004.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.max` when given `NaN` as an argument.
- **[15.08.02.11-005.js](15.08.02.11-005.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.max` returns `NaN` when one of its arguments is `NaN`.
- **[15.08.02.11-006.js](15.08.02.11-006.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of Math.max with NaN as an argument.
- **[15.08.02.11-007.js](15.08.02.11-007.js.md)**: Tests the Math.max function to verify it returns the maximum of two numbers.
- **[15.08.02.11-008.js](15.08.02.11-008.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.max()` returns `NaN`, which is not finite.
- **[15.08.02.11-009.js](15.08.02.11-009.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of Math.max() with no arguments, expecting -Infinity.
- **[15.08.02.11-010.js](15.08.02.11-010.js.md)**: Tests that Math.max() without arguments does not return Infinity.
- **[15.08.02.11-011.js](15.08.02.11-011.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of Math.max with positive and negative zero.
- **[15.08.02.11-012.js](15.08.02.11-012.js.md)**: Tests if `Math.max` is a function.
- **[15.08.02.11-013.js](15.08.02.11-013.js.md)**: Tests that the `Math.max` function has a length property equal to 2.
- **[15.08.02.11-014.js](15.08.02.11-014.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.max` with positive and negative zero.
